---
title: Predcting aboveground biomass density according to LiDAR product within BIOMASS R package
author: "D. Lamonica, A. Bailly, M. Réjou-Méchain"
institute: "UMR AMAP, IRD"
date: "January 30, 2026"
format: 
  beamer:
    slide-level: 2
    theme: default
    colortheme: seagull
header-includes: 
- \titlegraphic{\hspace{-8mm}\includegraphics[width=5cm]{img/logo_esa.jpg}\hspace{-3mm}\includegraphics[width=3cm]{img/logo.png}\hspace{5mm}\includegraphics[width=3cm]{img/logo_IRD.png}}
- \setbeamertemplate{section page}{\centering\begin{beamercolorbox}[sep=12pt,center,colsep=-4bp,rounded=true,shadow=true]{part title}\usebeamerfont{section title}\insertsection\par\end{beamercolorbox}}
- \setbeamertemplate{footline}[page number]
---

## BIOMASS R package FRM4BIOMASS

![](img/workflow_BIOMASS_v7_filtre.png){height=100%}

## \textcolor{violet}{CHM-AGBD model calibration (V3)}

![](img/workflow_BIOMASS_v7.png){height=100%}


## CHM-AGBD model calibration: proposed statistical framework

- geostatistical model with SPV-I/C (SPatially Varying Intercept/Coefficients) to integrate spatial correlation:

- $y(s) = (\alpha + \tilde{\alpha}(s)) + (\beta + \tilde{\beta}(s)) \times x(s) + \epsilon(s)$
\newline
with $\tilde{\alpha}(s_1),...,\tilde{\alpha}(s_n) \sim MVN(0,C_{\alpha}(s_i,s_j))$

- references
![](img/babcock.png){width=50%}![](img/hunka.png){width=50%}

## CHM-AGBD model calibration: proposed statistical framework

$y(s) = (\alpha + \tilde{\alpha}(s)) + (\beta + \tilde{\beta}(s)) \times x(s) + \epsilon(s)$
\newline
with $\tilde{\alpha}(s_1),...,\tilde{\alpha}(s_n) \sim MVN(0,C_{\alpha}(s_i,s_j))$


![](img/hunka_alpha.png){width=30%}![](img/hunka_matern.png){width=30%}
![](img/hunka_alphatilde.png){width=30%}

## CHM-AGBD model calibration: proposed statistical framework

- SPDE procedure

- semi-automated workflow in a github repository

- INLA based no R package on CRAN

$\rightarrow$ need for our own workflow

## Implementation challenges

- taking into account AGBD uncertainties

- computation time

- packages on CRAN

$\rightarrow$ we chose brms R package \hspace{2em} ![](img/brms.png){height=15%} 


## CHM-AGBD model calibration: example with simulated data

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
![](img/chm_brut.png){height=80%} 
:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

- SpVC model \newline
$log(AGBD(s)) = \alpha  + (\beta + \tilde{\beta}(s)) \times log(CHM(s)) + \epsilon(s)$
\newline
with $\tilde{\beta}(s_1),...,\tilde{\beta}(s_n) \sim MVN(0,C_{\beta}(s_i,s_j))$ and $\alpha = 0$

\vspace{0.5cm}

- Simulated data

![](img/mesh_simul_data.pdf){height=60%}

:::

::::


## CHM-AGBD model estimates brms

![](img/post_simul_brms.pdf){height=100%}

## CHM-AGBD covariance functions

![](img/cov_simul_brms.pdf){height=100%}

## CHM-AGBD posterior predictive checks

![](img/ppc_simul_brms.pdf){height=100%}



## CHM-AGBD model calibration: example with Nouragues data

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
![](img/nouragues_data.png){height=80%} 

:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

- SPV-C model \newline
$log(AGBD(s)) = (\beta + \tilde{\beta}(s)) \times log(CHM(s)) + \epsilon(s)$
\newline
with $\tilde{\beta}(s_1),...,\tilde{\beta}(s_n) \sim MVN(0,C_{\beta}(s_i,s_j))$

:::

::::


## Predictions on Nouragues (brms)
![](img/fitted_data_brms.pdf){height=58%}

## Predictions on Nouragues: coefficient of variation (brms)
![](img/fitted_data_brms2.pdf){height=58%}

## Two functions in the package
![](img/implem_rfunctions.png){height=100%}


## CHM-AGBD model calibration: implementation possibilities

- propagation of AGBD estimates uncertainties

- SPDE procedure

- future statistical development to use all the CHM spatial structure: better use of available information for a more robust & precise full spatial AGBD prediction (for a next major version)
